briefdescription.para: Get the block size (number of weights per block) for a quant type.
Calculate total bytes needed for n_elements with given quant type.
Get the byte size per block for a quant type.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B_q5, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Batch GEMM with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 activations for prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Computes C = A @ B^T + bias where: A: [M x K] Q8_0 quantized activations (M tokens, K features) B: [N x K] Q5_0 quantized weights (N outputs, K features) C: [M x N] FP32 output
This is the INT8 batch kernel for prefill, using pre-quantized activations to avoid FP32->Q8_0 conversion overhead per operation.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations in Q8_0 format [M rows of K/32 blocks each]
Weights in Q5_0 format [N rows of K/32 blocks each]
Optional bias vector [N], NULL if not used
Output matrix [M x N], row-major FP32

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_sse_v2

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q6_k_ref

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *A, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q6_k_sse

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k_sse

briefdescription.para: Quantize FP32 to Q8_0 format (scalar reference)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, void *vy, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_q8_k_fused

detaileddescription.para:
Benefits:
Single pass over input data (reduces DRAM pressure)
Normalization results stay in registers for quantization
Keeps hot data in L1/L2 cache

briefdescription.para: Unpack Q4_K sub-block scales and mins.

detaileddescription.para:
scales
The packed scales[12] array from block_q4_K
sc
Output: 8 unpacked scale values (multiply by super-block d)
m
Output: 8 unpacked min values (multiply by super-block dmin)
This matches llama.cpp's get_scale_min_k4() function exactly. The 12-byte scales array layout:
bytes 0-3: 6-bit scales[0-3] (high 2 bits used for scales[4-7])
bytes 4-7: 6-bit mins[0-3] (high 2 bits used for mins[4-7])
bytes 8-11: low 4 bits for scales[4-7], high 4 bits for mins[4-7]

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch quantized dot product Q5_0 x Q8_0.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority:
AVX512 (best performance on modern Intel/AMD)
AVX (256-bit float ops, works on Sandy/Ivy Bridge and newer)
SSSE3 (128-bit fallback)
Reference scalar (last resort)

briefdescription.para: Quantization block structures for weight-only quantization.

detaileddescription.para: Defines block structures for various quantization formats used in LLM inference. Primary focus on Q4_K_M which is commonly used for LLM weight compression.
Block structures are compatible with llama.cpp/GGML for model loading.
